---
title: "Stata Users - Import"
description: |
  In this page we teach how to access LIS data in LISSY using Stata programming language.
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
source("../src/stata_machine.R")
library(Statamarkdown)
stataexe <- get_stata_machine_path()
knitr::opts_chunk$set(engine.path=list(stata=stataexe))
```

```{stata, echo = FALSE, results = "hide", collectcode = TRUE}
do "../src/lissyuse.do"
```

## Importing data in LISSY

LIS and LWS datasets are split by country, year, and level (household and individual). To load data in LISSY, we can use the following two methods.

### Method 1: `lissyuse`

Created by the LIS team, the `lissyuse` command was designed to load LIS and LWS data in LISSY in the most efficient way possible.

```{stata, eval =TRUE, echo=TRUE}
qui lissyuse, iso2(it) hvars(hid region_c dname dhi year) from(2014)
tab dname
sum 
```

<br>

Alternatively, the \`lws\` option must be added, when importing LWS datasets, toghether with a change in the <b>Project</b> dropdown menu. 

<br>

![](/images/project_lws.png){fig-align="center"}

```{stata, eval =TRUE, echo=TRUE}
qui lissyuse, iso2(it) hvars(hid region_c dname year dnw) lws
sum dnw
```
